items.title: ATSB investigates engine shut down in remote plane crash

children.content: The Australian Transport Safety Bureau releases its preliminary report into how a small aircraft crashed in mangroves, with five passengers and two pilots on board.

gallery.altText: A cessna plane wreckage with writing overtop of picture
A damaged cessna plane steering wheel

thumbnail.altText: A cessna plane wreckage with writing overtop of picture
A damaged cessna plane steering wheel

items.title: Mother shares incredible survival story after WA's strongest storm in 49 years

children.content: A driver says she almost died in a freak car accident on a highway in regional WA during Sunday's wild weather.

image.altText: A car with significant damage parked on the side of the road at night

items.title: Copper thieves are more brazen and taking exceptional risks, demolition experts say

children.content: Demolition sites are being stripped for copper at an escalating rate, according to a Perth demolition contractor, risking electrocution and asbestos exposure to sell the scrap metal on the black market.

items.title: Red skies, rainbows and a ravaging storm — WA's weather in May, captured by you

children.content: May delivered Western Australia bursts of sunshine, brief spells of rain and long stretches of dry skies that left many wondering when winter would finally arrive — until it did with a bang.

gallery.altText: A silhouette of teenage girls playing volleyball on the beach at sunset
A double rainbow rises up into the sky on the horizon off City Beach.
Water covers the river foreshore from a storm surge at Canning Beach Road in Applecross.

thumbnail.altText: A silhouette of teenage girls playing volleyball on the beach at sunset
A double rainbow rises up into the sky on the horizon off City Beach.
Water covers the river foreshore from a storm surge at Canning Beach Road in Applecross.

items.title: 'Extraordinary career': Hundreds farewell veteran MP at state funeral

children.content: Hundreds of mourners farewell WA's longest-serving parliamentarian, war hero Bill Grayden, at his state funeral in Perth.

image.altText: A portrait of Bill Grayden on a table during a state funeral.

items.title: Grieving mum's experience highlights need for regulation in funeral sector

children.content: A First Nations family is calling for changes to the "unregulated" funeral industry after complaints about a loved one's burial went nowhere.

image.altText: A woman unsmiling with grey hair.

items.title: Spotlight on Welcome to Country ceremonies in wake of Anzac Day controversy

children.content: Indigenous elders explain why they believe Welcome to Country ceremonies have faced opposition, and why abandoning the practice would create a cultural "abyss".

gallery.altText: An Indigenous man in traditional cultural dress at a smoking ceremony
Noongar elder Aunty Tina Hayden carries out a Welcome to Country
Children watch a welcome to country ceremony

thumbnail.altText: An Indigenous man in traditional cultural dress at a smoking ceremony
Noongar elder Aunty Tina Hayden carries out a Welcome to Country
Children watch a welcome to country ceremony

items.title: Australia pushing for treaty with Solomon Islands to counter China

children.content: Australia is pushing for a new treaty with Solomon Islands as the Pacific nation's new prime minister, Matthew Wale,
visits Canberra. Follow live.

items.title: Semaglutide is used for weight loss but may benefit kidneys and heart

children.content: Research shows semaglutide — commonly sold as Ozempic or Wegovy — can improve kidney function and survival rates for people with some chronic diseases, even if they have heart issues.

image.altText: Two boxes of medication side by side, labelled Ozempic and Wegovy

items.title: Australian-made rocket motor test fired for the first time

children.content: New vision has been released of the first test-firing, a step towards developing a greater Australian-made missile industry.

image.altText: A rocket booster igniting from an engine.

gallery.altText: A trail of fire appearing out of a rocket motor as it is shot.

thumbnail.altText: A trail of fire appearing out of a rocket motor as it is shot.

items.title: Emotional Ukrainian star not accepting silence from Russian players

children.content: Ukrainian star Marta Kostyuk says it is unacceptable for Russians to stay silent on the war, especially after Daria Kasatkina's defection to Australia.

image.altText: Marta Kostyuk wipes away a tear during an on-court interview at the French Open.

items.title: This rapper was having panic attacks on stage, until he transitioned

children.content: Poet, rapper, musician, playwright, and novelist, Kae Tempest is a prodigious polymath. It wasn't until he affirmed his gender that he connected with his true self.

image.altText: Close-up of Kae Tempest looking down at sunset, with a city skyline in silhouette behind them.

items.title: Strongest storm system in 49 years tears through Perth, southern WA

children.content: Tens of thousands of homes are left without power and the SES receives hundreds of calls for help, with the roofs torn off two apartment buildings, as WA's southern half is hammered by more severe weather.

articles.title: Lucky escape for pair as tree falls on car amid WA wild weather

children.content: Two people have had a lucky escape after a tree fell onto their car on a major highway in WA's South West, as strong winds lashed the region.

image.altText: Emergency vehicle and personel at the scene

gallery.altText: A badly damaged apartment block with its roof torn off and metal debris.
Four SES workers in orange hi-vis clothing work to repair the damaged roof of a house with a fallen tree lying on it.
A side fence lies on the ground next to a pool in the backyard of a house after being blown over in a storm.

thumbnail.altText: A badly damaged apartment block with its roof torn off and metal debris.
Four SES workers in orange hi-vis clothing work to repair the damaged roof of a house with a fallen tree lying on it.
A side fence lies on the ground next to a pool in the backyard of a house after being blown over in a storm.

items.title: Quiz: Hold on to your hat, we're off on a tour of the globe

children.content: The famous US road trip along Route 66 links which two cities? And which country eats the most garlic per person?

image.altText: A statue of a woman with a city in the background and a full moon above her head

items.title: Stars and Renegades merger opens the door for Bushrangers in BBL

children.content: The Bushrangers mascot could appear in the Big Bash League after Cricket Victoria confirmed plans for the merger of the Melbourne Stars and Renegades.

image.altText: a batter dressed in green hits the ball, while a keeper in red is behind him at the stumps

items.title: AI videos spread 'wrong impression' of WA's north, tourism operators say

children.content: A wave of AI-generated videos posted to social media causes a stir among tour operators in WA's north. They say the content does not accurately portray the region and threatens the preservation of Indigenous culture.

gallery.altText: A picture of a gorge-like landscape and a waterway on a mobile phone.

thumbnail.altText: A picture of a gorge-like landscape and a waterway on a mobile phone.

items.title: Minimum wage rises more than inflation in 'particularly challenging' decision

children.content: Australia's minimum wage will increase by 5.97 per cent, and minimum award workers will get a 4.75 per cent pay boost, in the Fair Work Commission's (FWC) annual wage review.

image.altText: Hands hold a mug under the spout of a coffee machine.

items.title: WA farmer braces for 'year of survival' as farm profits dive and costs bite

children.content: The ABARES report for the June quarter predicts national crop production will fall by more than 20 per cent, with profits down 70 per cent for broadacre farms.

items.title: Greens stalwart Bob Brown joins fight against small South West sand mine

children.content: The prominent environmentalist declares the sand mine project "an industrial nightmare" after visiting the area.

gallery.altText: Bob Brown photographed in Hobart, February 2017.
Three men standing in front of a green paddock with some trees. One man is holding a "protect our pits" poster
A road surrounded by forest. A car is moving by quickly.
